## Overview

An AI consulting engagement framework defines the structured approach for delivering AI strategy, implementation, and transformation projects to enterprise clients. It covers discovery, diagnosis, solution design, delivery, and value realization phases.

## Engagement Types

| Type | Duration | Deliverable | Value |
|------|----------|-------------|-------|
| AI Readiness Assessment | 2–4 weeks | Report + roadmap | Diagnostic |
| AI Strategy Workshop | 1–2 days | Strategy document | Alignment |
| Proof of Concept | 4–8 weeks | Working prototype | Validation |
| Full Implementation | 3–12 months | Production system | Transformation |
| Advisory Retainer | Ongoing | Monthly guidance | Continuous |

## Engagement Phases

### Phase 1: Discovery (Week 1–2)

Objective: Understand current state, constraints, and desired outcomes.

Activities:
- Stakeholder interviews (C-suite, department leads, technical team)
- Data asset inventory
- Current AI/ML capability assessment
- Business process mapping

Deliverables:
- Discovery summary report
- Problem hypothesis
- Engagement scope proposal

### Phase 2: Diagnosis (Week 2–4)

Objective: Identify root causes, opportunity sizing, and prioritization.

Activities:
- Data quality assessment
- Technical debt analysis
- AI use case scoring (RICE or impact/feasibility matrix)
- Benchmark against industry peers

Deliverables:
- Prioritized opportunity map
- Risk assessment
- Investment/ROI model

### Phase 3: Solution Design (Week 4–6)

Objective: Define the target architecture and implementation approach.

Activities:
- Reference architecture design
- Make-vs-buy analysis (build custom vs. commercial AI platform)
- Vendor evaluation (if applicable)
- Governance and ethics framework

Deliverables:
- Solution architecture document
- Implementation roadmap
- Change management plan

### Phase 4: Delivery (Variable)

Objective: Implement the agreed solution.

Activities:
- Agile sprints with bi-weekly demos
- Data pipeline construction
- Model development and validation
- Integration with enterprise systems
- User acceptance testing

Deliverables:
- Working system (POC or production)
- Technical documentation
- Runbook

### Phase 5: Value Realization

Objective: Measure impact and embed capability.

Activities:
- KPI measurement vs baseline
- Team training and knowledge transfer
- Lessons learned review
- Roadmap for next phase

Deliverables:
- Impact report
- Capability transfer documentation
- Next engagement proposal

## Engagement Governance

- Weekly status call with project sponsor
- Bi-weekly steering committee (scope, budget, timeline)
- Risk log maintained throughout engagement
- Change request process for scope changes

## Pricing Models

| Model | Best For | Risk |
|-------|---------|------|
| Fixed price | Well-defined scope | Consultant bears scope risk |
| Time & materials | Exploratory work | Client bears cost risk |
| Value-based | High-impact projects | Shared upside |
| Retainer | Ongoing advisory | Predictable for both |

## Conclusion

Successful AI consulting engagements depend on clear problem framing, measurable success criteria, and active client partnership. The consulting engagement framework ensures disciplined delivery — from discovery through value realization — while managing the inherent uncertainty of AI projects.
